The speed measurement from the radar device.
Expressed as a `Measurement<UnitSpeed>` from Foundation, allowing easy conversion
between different speed units (mph, kph, m/s, etc.).
MAC address of the device that produced this measurement, if available.
Useful for identifying which device sent the measurement when multiple devices
- measurement: The speed measurement from the device.
- macAddress: Optional MAC address of the measuring device.

conform to `Hashable`, implement the `hash(into:)` requirement instead.
The compiler provides an implementation for `hashValue` for you.

Represents the current state of the SmartCoach SDK session.
The session state tracks the SDK's connection status and associated device information.
Use ``SmartCoach/sessionStateStream()`` to observe state changes reactively, or
The state transitions typically follow this pattern:
If connection is lost unexpectedly, the state may transition to `reconnecting` (if auto-reconnect
- Parameter SmartCoachError?: Optional error that caused the disconnection.
`nil` indicates a normal disconnection initiated by the user.
Actively scanning for nearby SmartCoach devices.
- Parameter [SmartCoachRadar]: Array of discovered devices. Updated as new devices
are found or existing devices' signal strength changes.

- Parameter SmartCoachRadar: The device being connected to.
Successfully connected to a device and ready to start measurements.
Actively receiving measurement data from the device.
- Parameter SmartCoachRadar: The device providing measurements.
Attempting to reconnect to a previously connected device.
This state occurs when auto-reconnect is enabled and the connection was lost unexpectedly.
- Parameter SmartCoachRadar: The device being reconnected to.

Configuration options for SmartCoach device behavior.
Use this structure to customize how the SDK manages device connections, particularly
around reconnection behavior when connections are lost.
Determines whether the SDK automatically attempts to reconnect when connection is lost.
When `true`, the SDK will automatically try to reconnect to the last paired device
if the connection is unexpectedly lost. When `false`, you must manually call
- Background operations where user intervention isn't possible
- Apps where users frequently switch devices
- When you want full control over connection lifecycle
- Parameter autoReconnect: Whether to automatically reconnect when connection is lost.
A protocol representing a SmartCoach radar device.
This protocol defines the properties and capabilities of a SmartCoach device discovered
during scanning. Devices conforming to this protocol can be passed to ``SmartCoach/connect(to:)``
Received Signal Strength Indicator (RSSI) in dBm.
Higher values (closer to 0) indicate stronger signal. Typical range is -100 to 0.
MAC address of the device, if available.
The measurement unit configured on the device.
Current battery level of the device.
Current power source of the device.
Model number of the device, if available.
The measurement unit configured on a SmartCoach device.
Indicates whether the device displays speeds in miles per hour or kilometers per hour.

The main interface for interacting with SmartCoach devices.
`SmartCoach` provides a simple, static API for configuring the SDK, discovering devices,
establishing connections, and streaming measurement data. All methods are `@MainActor` isolated
and designed to be called from the main thread.
1. Configure the SDK once at app launch using ``configure(deviceConfigurationOptions:)``
Configures the SmartCoach SDK for use.
This method must be called once before using any other SDK features, typically during
your app's initialization. Calling this method multiple times will throw an error.
- Parameter deviceConfigurationOptions: Optional configuration for device behavior.
If `nil`, default options are used. See `SmartCoachDeviceConfigurationOptions` for available settings.
- Throws: `SmartCoachError` if configuration fails. Common errors include:
- `SmartCoachError.notConfigured`: SDK has not been configured yet
- `SmartCoachError.missingApiKey`: API key not found in Info.plist
- Important: This method must be called before any other SDK methods.
Starts scanning for nearby SmartCoach devices.
Initiates a Bluetooth scan to discover available SmartCoach devices in range. Optionally
attempts to automatically connect to the last successfully paired device.
- Parameter connectToLastPairedDevice: If `true`, automatically connects to the previously
paired device if found. If `false`, scanning continues until you manually call ``connect(to:)``.
- Throws: `SmartCoachError` if scanning fails. Common errors include:
- `SmartCoachError.notConfigured`: SDK was not configured before use
// Start scanning and auto-connect to last device
- Note: There is no need to call ``stopScanning()`` once a device is connected. The SDK handles that.
Halts the active Bluetooth scan. This should be called when the user cancels the device selection process.
- Throws: `SmartCoachError.notConfigured` if called before the SDK is configured.
// User decides not to connect to any device...
- Note: There is no need to call ``stopScanning()`` once a device is connected. The SDK handles that.
Connects to a specific SmartCoach device.
Establishes a Bluetooth connection to the specified device. The connection process may
take several seconds depending on signal strength and device availability.
- Parameter radar: The `SmartCoachRadar` device to connect to, typically obtained from
- Throws: `SmartCoachError` if connection fails. Common errors include:
Disconnects from the currently connected SmartCoach device.
Gracefully terminates the active Bluetooth connection. This method always succeeds and
does not throw errors. However, if an error occurs during disconnect, ``sessionStateStream()``
- Note: If auto-reconnect is enabled in configuration, the SDK will not attempt to reconnect automatically. That is only for unexpected disconnects.
Starts streaming measurement data from the connected device.
Begins receiving real-time measurement data from the SmartCoach device. The measurements
are delivered as an `AsyncStream` that continues until ``stopMeasuring()`` or ``disconnect()`` is called or
- Returns: An `AsyncStream<MeasurementData>` that yields measurement data as it becomes available.
- Throws: `SmartCoachError` if unable to start measurements. Common errors include:
- Important: Always call ``stopMeasuring()`` when done to free resources and conserve device battery.
Stops the measurement stream from the connected device.
Terminates the active measurement stream and releases associated resources. Any active
- Throws: `SmartCoachError.notConfigured` if called before the SDK is configured.
Creates a stream of session state changes.
Provides an `AsyncStream` that emits ``SmartCoachSessionState`` values whenever the SDK's
connection state changes (e.g., scanning, connecting, connected, measuring).
- Returns: An `AsyncStream<SmartCoachSessionState>` that yields state changes as they occur.
- Throws: `SmartCoachError.notConfigured` if called before the SDK is configured.
- Note: This stream continues indefinitely. Store the task and cancel it when no longer needed.
Provides immediate access to the SDK's current connection state without subscribing
- Note: For reactive state updates, use ``sessionStateStream()`` instead.
Error codes used by the SmartCoach SDK.
Each error code represents a specific failure condition and is organized into categories:
configuration errors (1000–1999), entitlement errors (3000–3999), and device errors (4000–4999).
Returns a Boolean value indicating whether two values are not equal.

The SDK has already been configured.
`configure()` can only be called once per app session.
The SDK has not been configured.
Call `configure()` before using any other SDK methods.
API key is missing from Info.plist.
Add a `SmartCoachAPIKey` entry to your Info.plist file.
The app's bundle identifier is invalid or missing.
Verify your bundle ID matches what's registered in the SmartCoach developer portal.
Check that all required Info.plist entries are present and valid.
The requested feature is not available with your current subscription.
Check your app's entitlements or upgrade your subscription.
Entitlements have expired or are invalid.
Ensure network access is available to refresh the token and verify your subscription status.
Bluetooth is not available on this device.
Bluetooth may be disabled, unauthorized, or unsupported on this device.
Failed to connect to the device.
The device may be out of range, already connected to another device, or powered off.
Connect to a device before attempting to start measurements.
The connection to the device was unexpectedly lost.
This may be due to distance, interference, or device battery issues.
The requested feature is not supported by this device.
Check device capabilities before attempting the operation.
Failed to start scanning for devices.
This is usually temporary. Try again after a short delay.
Ensure the device is connected and supports measurements.

An error returned by SmartCoach SDK operations.
`SmartCoachError` wraps a ``SmartCoachErrorCode`` and provides additional context through
the `domain` property. Use pattern matching with ``SmartCoachErrorMatcher`` for clean error handling.
